summary.intent: Refactor gossamer from an Electron desktop app to a VS Code extension to reduce install friction (auto-detect .entire/settings.json instead of manual repo registration) and improve context-switching between editing and viewing Claude sessions.
summary.outcome: A fully functional VS Code extension was built with sessions list, session detail view, native checkpoint tree sidebar, MCP server embedded at /mcp, search via QuickPick with FTS, image rendering, agent-aware labels and commands, VSIX packaging script, and onboarding flow for new users.

repo: Each repo in ~/.gossamer/config.json gets its own SQLite database at dbPath; the server reads this at startup and uses the correct per-repo DB
Entire.io writes session data to the `entire/checkpoints/v1` git branch and shadow branches; gossamer indexes from git into SQLite, never directly from Entire.io
Session naming uses `custom-title` events stored in ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-path>/*.jsonl files, NOT the `slug` field on LogEvent (slug is auto-generated and never changes on /rename)
Images are stored as base64 in full.jsonl files on the checkpoint branch under content blocks with type='image' and source.data/source.media_type fields
The server process runs from gossamer-testing/dist/serve.js (the worktree) when using F5 in the extension dev host, not from gossamer/dist/ — all rebuilds must happen in the worktree

better-sqlite3 native module version mismatch with VS Code's bundled Electron Node.js (MODULE_VERSION 140 vs 141); solved by spawning the server as a child process using system node (`which node`) instead of importing Prisma in the extension host
Agent name comes from CheckpointSessionMetadata.agent; session color is not persisted anywhere in Claude's data files
acquireVsCodeApi() can only be called once per webview; must store as window.__vscodeApi and reuse in vscodeApi.ts

code.finding: Original config listed only src/extension.ts and src/GossamerPanel.ts in `include`, causing SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts to silently never compile. Fix: use `include: ["src/**/*.ts"]` with `exclude: ["src/webview"]`
Server is a static singleton (GossamerPanel.server, GossamerPanel.serverPort) that survives panel disposal; disposeWebviewOnly() hides the panel without killing the server, enabling instant restore
getConfiguredPort() reads ~/.gossamer/config.json for the port; DEFAULT_PORT=3456 is the stable fallback, replacing getFreePort() which was random and made MCP config impossible
Checks window.__vscodeApi first before calling acquireVsCodeApi() to handle the case where the host page script already acquired it
root must use resolve(__dirname, 'src/webview') with absolute path; relative path resolves from CWD (repo root when run via workspace), not package directory
onDidChangeViewState listener calls checkpointProvider.setSession() when webviewPanel.active — required to update checkpoint sidebar when switching between open session detail tabs

imageData String? and imageMediaType String? were added to LogContent model; existing rows required a manual backfill from LogEvent.data JSON column since old rows were indexed before the schema change
Webview HTML CSP requires `img-src data: https: http:` to render base64 images; default-src 'none' blocks all data: URIs

workflow: gossamer-testing worktree pattern: edit source in gossamer/, manually copy changed files to gossamer-testing/, rebuild extension there; all F5 test runs use gossamer-testing built artifacts
VS Code webviews reload when dragged to a new window; use panel.onDidChangeViewState to re-send server_ready or other state messages when the panel becomes visible again
fs.watch on .db/.db-wal files and .git/refs/ with debouncing replaces polling for near-real-time updates; keep 30s fallback polls for reliability

Use TextDocumentContentProvider with a custom URI scheme (gossamer-diff:) to serve in-memory diff content instead of writing temp files; content provider approach is cleaner and files are inherently read-only
When a TypeScript build succeeds but runtime behavior is wrong, check which dist/ directory the running process is actually loading from — often a stale artifact from a different location
For per-repo state (checkpoint tree, session focus), use TreeDataProvider.setSession() called from panel.onDidChangeViewState rather than only on panel creation

friction: gossamer-testing worktree requires manual file syncing (cp) after every edit in the main repo; easy to forget and waste time debugging stale behavior
Server runs from gossamer-testing/dist/ not gossamer/dist/ — caused multiple debugging sessions where code changes appeared to have no effect
tsconfig.extension.json silently excluded new files, causing blank diff views with no error message until traced to the missing compiled output
VS Code's bundled Node.js has a different MODULE_VERSION than system node, causing native module crashes; required full architecture change to external process
Prisma schema pushes failed with FTS table conflicts on existing DBs; had to fall back to raw SQLite ALTER TABLE commands
acquireVsCodeApi() called twice when minimize button inline script and React app both called it — required window.__vscodeApi singleton pattern
Ctrl+K G chord keybinding does not fire when the webview has keyboard focus, making the toggle command unreliable
Image backfill required a one-time Python script to populate imageData from the raw LogEvent.data JSON column for sessions indexed before the schema change
open_items: gossamer.vsix and packages/vscode/bundled-server/serve.js are generated artifacts that should be added to .gitignore
The gossamer-testing worktree file sync is manual and error-prone; a watch script or rsync hook would eliminate most of the sync friction
Ctrl+K G toggle panel keybinding doesn't work when webview has focus — needs an alternative trigger (e.g., status bar button or command from within the webview)
Session color (chosen in Claude Code UI) is not persisted anywhere in Claude's data files — cannot be implemented without Entire.io storing it in the checkpoint metadata
Claude output images (tool results with image content) are not yet rendered — only user-input images were implemented; requires checking if tool_result content blocks contain image types
The rewindToCheckpoint command uses isAgentRunning() which checks ~/.claude/sessions/*.json — this may not work for non-Claude agents (Gemini, Codex)
AGENT_CLI mapping in GossamerPanel.ts only covers Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex — other agents will fall back to claude binary which may be wrong

The summary.txt virtual file shows the full Entire.io summary; if Entire.io summaries are not enabled, the file only shows basic checkpoint metadata — consider indicating to the user how to enable summaries
MCP server embed uses stateless StreamableHTTPServerTransport; if Claude Code adds session support for stateful MCP connections, the transport config will need updating
